title: 3D Flip, Split Screen, Extrusion, Ripple, Split-Flap
description: Five live UI demos: scroll-driven 3D card entrances, diagonal split layout, CSS text-shadow 3D extrusion, click ripple rings, and a Solari split-flap board.

drops.title: Scroll-Driven 3D Entrance
drops.description: Cards flip from a rotated 3D state into view as they scroll into the viewport — pure CSS, zero JavaScript.
drops.concept: Uses CSS animation-timeline: view() with animation-range: entry to bind a 3D rotateY/X flip animation to viewport entry. No IntersectionObserver needed. Three flip variants (Y-axis, X-axis, combined) demonstrate the technique across different card positions.
drops.source: CSS scroll-driven animations spec (W3C) + MDN documentation

drops.title: Diagonal Split Screen
drops.description: Two full-screen panels split by a diagonal divider that the user can drag — powered entirely by CSS clip-path: polygon().
drops.concept: Both panels sit full-screen and overlapping; a CSS custom property --x drives the clip-path polygon on each panel so they perfectly complement each other. Dragging the handle updates --x in real-time. A 'flip slant' button reverses the diagonal direction.

drops.title: 3D Extrusion Text
drops.description: Stacked text-shadow layers create convincing 3D depth — hover lifts the text, revealing the full extrusion in four style variants.
drops.concept: CSS text-shadow accepts multiple comma-separated values; by stepping them 1px diagonally with progressively darker hues, you build a convincing 3D block extrusion with zero additional markup. Hover transitions shift the shadow stack and translate the text for a satisfying lift effect.

interaction.mouse: Hover each text block to lift it

drops.title: Click Ripple Rings
drops.description: Clicking anywhere spawns expanding concentric rings with a particle burst — three modes: classic rings, filled ripple, multi-ring shockwave.
drops.concept: Each click dynamically creates ring-shaped divs positioned at the click coordinates, animated with CSS @keyframes to expand and fade. A Web Animations API particle burst adds kinetic feedback. The multicolor mode cycles through a palette per click.

drops.title: Split-Flap Display
drops.description: A faithful mechanical Solari departure board — each character tile cycles through random glyphs before landing on the target, with a live clock.
drops.concept: Each tile uses CSS 3D rotateX to simulate a split-flap flip mid-animation, swapping the character content at the 90-degree point. JavaScript chains async flip sequences tile-by-tile with slight stagger, exactly replicating how real Solari boards update. Hover a row to re-scramble; auto-update fires every 8 seconds.
drops.source: Solari Udine mechanical split-flap display concept

interaction.mouse: Hover a row to scramble it
